issues.title: Initial Discussion Notes
issues.author: johngray-dev
issues.body:
Here is combined notes from Mike Ounsworths and myself:


Attendees: John Gray, Luke (NCSC), DavidBen, Bas, Jan Klaußner


David:  https://www.chromium.org/Home/chromium-security/post-quantum-auth-roadmap/

Discussion on Use Cases:

High volume (batching)
- Batch signing, if you're concerned about HSM capacity

High auditability
- PKIs that issue admin credentials?
- A more efficient and flexible transparency/auditing scheme, if you either already use CT or would have found auditing schemes compelling if CT worked differently 
- Auditing case, private PKI's audited to the hilt.   Work by dumping something out of a database.  Extra machinery is worth it if you don't trust the operator of the PKI. 
- System of building trusting... Can trust be reduced to automation.

Allowing things to stay on RSA / ECC
- Imagine an industrial IoT setting where things want to continue using ECDSA certs for size reasons. Imagine putting those into an MTC and having a witness that monitors for suspicious activity adjacent to the closed IoT network continuing to use the legacy certs.

Use Case for Transport / Admin PKI
- Admin plane won't be high volume, but it needs to be high assurance.  Secret admin credential

Downgrade protection (using transparency logs)
- Have to turn off quantum vulnerable algorithms but still service quantum vulnerable certs.   Leads to a downgrade...
- Quantum capable client, server has both quantum and non-quantum...  Way to solve this in 2 ways.  HSTS is one way to fix.
- Second way is to use transparency auditing - then you can check by using the log
https://westerbaan.name/~bas/rwpqc2026/bas.pdf

Multiple co-signers
- Basically anything that uses Timestamp Authorities (RFC3161) today could map to this.
- eIDAS and ICAO ePassport

PQC Migration:
- Possible use of migration?   Downgrade attacks?   CA service provider.  
- Way to get web PKI off classic roots

eIDAS - ICAO (passports)
- Jan mentioned this as a use-case

Landmark build-up:
- Imagine a smartcard building access door reader. It needs to be able to accept any employee ID badge issued in the past 10 years. This device will have limited on-board memory. Today, it has the root cert, and just needs to fetch today’s CRL (then throw out yesterday’s CRL). This use case wants MTC to have a landmark scheme where the new landmark replaces the previous landmark.
- We should provide guidance for how to calculate the bound (ceiling) on how much disk space is required for a Verifier to successfully be able to validate all certificates issued in a X day window.

Landmark build-up:
- Imagine a smartcard building access door reader. It needs to be able to accept any employee ID badge issued in the past 10 years. This device will have limited on-board memory. Today, it has the root cert, and just needs to fetch today’s CRL (then throw out yesterday’s CRL). This use case wants MTC to have a landmark scheme where the new landmark replaces the previous landmark.
- We should provide guidance for how to calculate the bound (ceiling) on how much disk space is required for a Verifier to successfully be able to validate all certificates issued in a X day window.


General
- Timestamp authority, counter-signer - anything that uses TSA's could be a target for multi-signed MTC
- The landmark certificates optimization, if (a subset of) your RPs are able to manage it
- Might be use-cases without connection to update service..  Has access to a mechanism to fetch landmarks.
- Server could send landmarks to client if they don't have them.
- Longer cert lifetimes - client will have to store more landmarks.   
- Create a scheme for rebasing the landmarks?  
- How to re-anchor the card once it is out.
- Can we make a MT of the landmarks?
- Need a fetching mechanism - like CRL's
- There is a fall back mode...  
- What are the options for making use of this new tool, and what is the advantages
- Zero trust work-from-home, continuous authentication - new VPN connection every so often.

- Rebase only if you are not happy with the rebase - maybe tuning parameters
- You need to allocate this much disk space -  10 years * 365 landmarks * 32


John / Bas Conversations (April 22nd, 2026)
- Maybe we start simple - a Certificate Verifier for clients which use the MTC landmarks.   Non web PKI's will likely come across MTC and need to know how to verify.
- A mechanism to fetch landmarks (could be like a CRL mechanism).   Maybe an LDP (Landmark Distribution point)? 
- a new purpose intent for draft-lamps-certificate-discovery?  Or just define separately
